page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration allocates 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile for multi-core operations, though single-core performance is relatively limited. The stress-ng single-core CPU benchmark registers in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show a stark contrast: small-block cached reads perform at an average level of 800.3 GB/sec, but large-block uncached reads and writes drop to the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Despite these memory throughput constraints, the instance maintains average performance in data compression, decompression, and Redis SET operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6a.4xlarge provides a dense allocation of dedicated physical cores without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a viable option for workloads that require sustained multi-threaded CPU capacity but do not demand high single-core speeds or massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, intermediate database operations, and general-purpose compute tasks that align with its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, it provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio without bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals average multi-core CPU performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations, though single-core CPU performance and uncached large-block memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core speeds. It is well-suited for web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and intermediate database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.

tagline: A dedicated multi-core compute instance featuring a balanced memory profile and stable network throughput for parallel processing workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and Redis database performance
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware included
Suitable for static web serving and multi-threaded data processing
